Gnome 2026-01-30 9:40 a.m.

Gallie_2k 2026-01-30 9:47 a.m.
I used this website https://www.heywhatsthat.com/ And put a location near me in and the allowed it to find the right area in 1 mile radius so it's decent for catching down into Hartlepool and also down to Stockton. I was surprised by the distance cross country it catch Manchester well which connects us to the main mesh YM-* being the main hope it seems to most things
HeyWhatsThat
Compute summits visible from any location worldwide

EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-20 1:22 p.m.
Hi all. I just got my first radio today, a Heltec v3. I'm in Gateshead near the stadium but I have no contacts. Is this area generally pretty quiet?
EvilBlueSmartie
Hi all. I just got my first radio today, a Heltec v3. I'm in Gateshead near the stadium but I have no contacts. Is this area generally pretty quiet?
Fireball 2026-02-21 4:11 a.m.
aye unfortunately i dont think theres much linking you to the mesh there. I live down in teesside but work in newcastle so i'm up and down the a19 regular. There's a couple of guys out in durham and one in hartlepool, then its kind of dead north of that. Always people popping up though so worth sticking with it. We're a bit isolated at the moment as the main solar node in catterick that keeps us on the UK mesh is struggling with the winter
EvilBlueSmartie
Hi all. I just got my first radio today, a Heltec v3. I'm in Gateshead near the stadium but I have no contacts. Is this area generally pretty quiet?
jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt) 2026-02-21 1:09 p.m.
We have a repeater which went up today in NE11, it is incredibly quiet around here in Gateshead... The map shows somebody advertising a repeater called "MetroCentreRepeater" but it appears down at the moment
EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-21 1:11 p.m.
I'll fire my radio up again today then and see if I can pick it up.
1:12 p.m.

EvilBlueSmartie

EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-21 3:06 p.m.
Alright, I'm back up and set to UK Narrow. Still no hits. Should I expect to be able to see the other nodes nearby with Heltec v3, or is the range not good enough for that?
Fireball 2026-02-21 3:07 p.m.
with the new firmware theres an option to be a companion and repeater at the same time but i think its bugged as i cant enable it on mine. UK narrow yeah thats the one you want The range is mostly line of sight so if there's noone nearby on top of a hill to get you out you'll struggle
EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-21 3:08 p.m.
Yeah I think there's a big hill between me and NE11 so that'll be it I imagine.
3:09 p.m.
I'm thinking of taking it to work with me on Monday. My office is at the top of a tall building in the city centre so I may have more luck there. Might be handy to keep a repeater on my desk there.
Fireball 2026-02-21 3:11 p.m.
could be worth a punt i havent picked much of anything up while up there in the office so it'd be interesting to see if we can get anything out
EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-21 3:13 p.m.
I'll let you know when it's there then and we can see. I only have the one radio for now, but if it picks something up I may get another and see if I can hit it from home.
Fireball 2026-02-21 3:13 p.m.
its a slippery slope, there's still time to save yourself while you only have one!

EvilBlueSmartie

I'm going out with my mobile rig tomorrow to see if I can see anything around this side of Gateshead
4:37 p.m.

Fireball 2026-02-21 4:58 p.m.
aye thats our big struggle, being down in a valley is a nightmare for radio

This wide band/narrow band issue is also a problem... this is what I'm facing. The blue circles are narrow band, the red circles are wide band.
1:36 a.m.
I literally can't connect to my nearest neighbours because a decision was made to change from the default settings and there is no way I can inform my neighbours that they need to change their settings
jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt)
I literally can't connect to my nearest neighbours because a decision was made to change from the default settings and there is no way I can inform my neighbours that they need to change their settings
Fireball 2026-02-23 3:32 a.m.
there's quite a lot on the map that no longer exist, even if someones plugged it in for 5 mins to test it then never turned it on again it can still show up (if you click on them it shows the date added / last updated). It defaults to narrow now so in theory "new" people should find us but the old firmware defaulted to long range meaning people never got any connections
jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt) 2026-02-23 4:26 a.m.
Do you know what version of the firmware Narrow was made default in? Because these repeaters were just added last month and why would you use old firmware for a new repeater?
Fireball 2026-02-23 4:30 a.m.
i think it was october time so 1.10. Like i say its kind of misleading as all you would need to do is plug in an old board and it would show up. For example i have a v3 here that i have no idea what firmware is on it. If i plugged it in to flash it to meshtastic or whatever, as long as it got an advert out before the flash started it could show up on the map then never be seen again
4:32 a.m.
prime example, i only have 2 repeaters up but there are 4 still showing on the map from when they've been flashed and messed about with
EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-23 4:55 a.m.
I flashed my v3 a few days ago and it was wide band by default.
Caoboth 2026-02-23 4:55 a.m.
must admit mines on the latest firmware as did an OTA update before i put it on the roof. Other thing i was curious about was prefernace on antenna ive got a 40cm 5.5dbi antenna on mine atm (wonder if i should be upping that to a 8DBi one to max the distance
4:56 a.m.
that being said its all new to me so i could be completly wrong

Dayle - M0OUE 2026-02-23 5:24 a.m.
If it's a companion, in the app go to the 3 dots at the top right > tools > discover. Normally you will only see new nodes when they advert every 12 hours or so but the discover feature allows you to send out ping and they will respond. (edited)
5:25 a.m.
It only works directly though so you can't send a discover ping through a repeater on a roof for exanple

EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-24 9:03 a.m.
So what determines the strength of an antenna? I know dBi sort of determines the shape of the signal, so a higher dBi can reach further. Is a physically larger 3dBi antenna typically more powerful than a smaller 3dBi antenna, or are there other metrics we can use? And how do you go about powering a larger antenna, as I imagine you need more power to transmit further.

Robin - M0JQQ - IO95ec 2026-02-24 10:46 a.m.
A vertical antenna is essentially omnidirectional. imagine a slightly flattened ball with the antenna as the axis. As you increase the antenna gain, the ball gets squashed top and bottom into a donut shape at first, progressing to a fat disk at the higher gains. At low gain your Tx goes out in all directions (Rx comes in from all directions too). You will also get out to receivers which are both higher and lower than your antenna. As you increase antenna gain the donut shape reduces sensitivity of those higher and lower stations. So it is always a compromise.
1

I'll let you know when I have everything ready and we can give it a try.

Robin - M0JQQ - IO95ec
A vertical antenna is essentially omnidirectional. imagine a slightly flattened ball with the antenna as the axis. As you increase the antenna gain, the ball gets squashed top and bottom into a donut shape at first, progressing to a fat disk at the higher gains. At low gain your Tx goes out in all directions (Rx comes in from all directions too). You will also get out to receivers which are both higher and lower than your antenna. As you increase antenna gain the donut shape reduces sensitivity of those higher and lower stations. So it is always a compromise.
EvilBlueSmartie 2026-02-24 11:55 a.m.
Thank you.

Bye Andy 2026-03-03 5:15 p.m.

5:18 p.m.

Bye Andy
im currently (I mean claude) is trying to make a more accurate prediction algorithm on the packet paths
NE35 2026-03-03 5:28 p.m.
Why do you need to predict it? Don't they carry the path info in the advertisement? Like how you can view it in the chat. Won't getting other MQTT nodes linked in give that info, similar to how Meshcore analyzer handles it.
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 5:30 p.m.
yeah the packets give each hop using the first 2 characters of the hex of the repeater, many repeaters have the same first 2 characters, wich is as far as im aware is how lets mesh does it too which is why you see the really long paths that are impossible
5:30 p.m.
thats your message using the accurate paths
5:31 p.m.
it starts at a repeater where we dont have duplicates for the first two hex characters then works from there checking on each hop if the repeater is 'in range' of the next one it thinks
1
5:37 p.m.
only thing thats stopping my system from working is I have about 1/8th of the actual repeaters in the UK

NE35 2026-03-03 6:33 p.m.
What do these mean? Are they hops because yours are pretty high if they are
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:33 p.m.
yeah they are hops
NE35 2026-03-03 6:33 p.m.
How do you get 21 if it's not exiting Teesside?
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:33 p.m.
its a recieved packet
6:34 p.m.
the 1tx is my node transmitting that packet once
NE35 2026-03-03 6:35 p.m.
Oh, the packet size?
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:35 p.m.
tbf all the packets on the website rn are just ones ive recieved, meshcore doesnt get the same repeater to forward a packet twice
NE35 2026-03-03 6:35 p.m.
I'm new to this terminology I apologise
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:36 p.m.
its the amount of other repeaters that have had the message before mine, meshcore appends the packet with the first two characters of your repeaters public key before sending it on, its how you see how many hops there are by counting how many 2 character hex's there are in that section of the packet
6:37 p.m.
so you see on the bit at the top where it has path, it corelates to the part in green which is the whole packet
NE35 2026-03-03 6:37 p.m.
But there aren't 21 repeaters in Teesside and we aren't leaving here that's what's I'm confused about.
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:38 p.m.
ah so its a packet that was outside teesside
NE35 2026-03-03 6:38 p.m.
Or is it bouncing back and forth between them?
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:38 p.m.
so 20 repeaters before it hit mine were outside teesside
NE35 2026-03-03 6:38 p.m.
Ooh ok.
6:38 p.m.
I saw your name and thought it was one you sent
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:39 p.m.
nah haha, its all the packets recieved by mine basically right now
6:39 p.m.
it will change when I get more people on the MQTT so I dont know how im gonna handle that
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:46 p.m.
over 400 active repeaters have been logged on the site (some dont show on the map which is why its lower on there as they have the sign)
6:47 p.m.
or ones that have no coordinate
NE35 2026-03-03 6:49 p.m.
It's more than the 350 in the app, so it should fill out over time
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 6:49 p.m.
also the 3 next to adverts for repeaters is the amount of times its been heard, if its new it just says new
mrlm 2026-03-03 6:56 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-03 7:31 p.m.
It uses https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viterbi_algorithm
Viterbi algorithm
The Viterbi algorithm is a dynamic programming algorithm that finds the most likely sequence of hidden events that would explain a sequence of observed events. The result of the algorithm is often called the Viterbi path. It is most commonly used with hidden Markov models (HMMs). For example, if a doctor observes a patient's symptoms over severa...
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 7:32 p.m.
time to get researching lol
mrlm 2026-03-03 7:35 p.m.
I know what it does and that's it. I don't claim to understand it or a lot of other things that have come up in discussion while I have been helping. but in simples terms they way i thought of doing it is whe these complex wasy are doing. (edited)
Bye Andy 2026-03-03 7:35 p.m.
fair enough, I feel its not an impossible task trying to figure out the route of a meshcore packet with somewhat high accuracy
mrlm 2026-03-03 7:39 p.m.
Some of these routes here from London to the south coast go over challenging high ground and there is an insistence they are incorrect. But the packet was first heard by an observer in London and last heard by one in Eastbourne, so it is hard to logically claim it took an alternative route.
7:40 p.m.
I thought maybe it is reflection off aircraft coming into land at one of the many nearby airports, but we cannot prove it either way at the moment. Multibyte https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/discussions/1613#discussioncomment-15783041 will be here soon and sort it. (edited)
The Case for Expanding Repeater IDs Beyond 1 Byte · meshcore-dev M...
As MeshCore networks grow and cities start connecting to each other, the 1-byte repeater ID (00–FF) is becoming a real pain point. This isn&#39;t a theoretical future problem, it&#39;s something co...

yeah that would really help
7:47 p.m.

7:48 p.m.

7:48 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-03 7:49 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-04 8:01 a.m.

NE35 2026-03-04 8:01 a.m.

mrlm 2026-03-04 8:04 a.m.
Use the webflasher to erase the device but dont flash it. Disconnect and restart it. Close everything, just open device manager. Connect and see it appear. Open browser go to meshrank observer setup again. Erase again when flashing.

mrlm 2026-03-04 8:12 a.m.

NE35 2026-03-04 8:13 a.m.

mrlm 2026-03-04 8:13 a.m.

NE35 2026-03-04 5:05 p.m.

Bye Andy 2026-03-04 5:05 p.m.
Packet paths are just finding the closest repeater with the matching first 2 hex characters, beta paths are a way more complex algorithm which is why you dont see full paths sometimes

EvilBlueSmartie 2026-03-04 5:49 p.m.
I'd be looking at pulling stuff out of App.tsx into distinct components, hooks etc. You have a lot of state there too that could either be scoped to the components that use it (if not shared) or wrapped in a context provider. Things like the SVGs and that can be very easily moved into separate components, for example. For an application of this size, App.tsx should just be an entry point really, not where all your business logic and state lives.

Bye Andy 2026-03-04 8:59 p.m.
Forwarded
I know what your probably thinking, why do we need another packet analyzer. Well I started it as a local project for Teesside in the UK, but as we started receiving packets from the wider mesh again it started to really expand so I made the project UK wide, you can check it out on https://www.ukmesh.com, the live map is what has had the most work done to it. This is purely data that has arrived to my node in Teesside , so the data is very heavily skewed so shortly im going to be opening it up to some select areas around the UK to see how the site copes. The part of my project that I think really improves on prior maps is the links it can draw between nodes, it seems quite accurate so far! One of the things on my list is to improve the coverage map filter massively, I am aware how inaccurate the method is that I am using currently. its also open source on github at https://github.com/gadgethd/teesside-mesh im unsure on how well it will build on different machines but that is something we can get to when the time comes. *Also yes this project is quite similar to meshrank.net I am aware, I only found that site after starting mine up Anyway its very late here in the UK when im posting this so ill be going to bed
Originally sent: 2026-03-04 8:59 p.m.

NE35 2026-03-05 9:03 a.m.

jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt)
I'm just using a spare Heltec v3 for now, but will put a heatsink on it because my loft can get very warm in the summer... Will need a case that can accommodate the heltec and heatsink
mrlm 2026-03-05 11:27 a.m.
You don't need to worry about a heat sink on the board, they can cope with >85C. If you have a battery you want to keep that below 45C which may be pushing it in a loft on the worst days. Ideally if it is plugged in and you just want a more stable supply you would use a Lithium-ion capacitor. With USB power v3 needed this v4 I think is ok without.
Caoboth
Well thats the NE38 repeater switched from a heltec v3 to a v4 and the tx set to 30 so hopefully a bit more reach
mrlm 2026-03-05 11:28 a.m.
set tx 30 does nothing, the maximum the radio chip will accept is 22. If you enter set tx 1 then set tx 30 followed by get tx it will say 30 but be set to 1.
11:29 a.m.
The plastic antenna on Amazon etc could have anything inside. This is the kind you want really https://www.ebay.co.uk/itm/297107020550 Cheaper ones I believe are fine https://www.ebay.co.uk/itm/266362872108
6dBI Helium Antenna Tuned McGill Microwave High Gain N Male EU 868 ...
New Optimised Performance Helium Network Antennas from McGill Microwave Systems Ltd. This new Antenna design has been specifically tuned to operate with peak performance over only the frequency band of interest to UK/EU Helium Hotspot operators – the UK/EU 868 frequency band.
Fibreglass Antenna EU tuned 3dBi 4dBi 5.8dBi 6dBi 8dBi 10dBi 12dBi ...
3dBi / 4dBi / 5.8dBi / 6dBi/ 8dBi /10dBi / 12dBi. For Helium Miner (HNT). High Quality Fiberglass (HNT) Antenna EU tuned (868MHz Frequency). This range of Antennas design has been specifically tuned to operate with peak performance over only the frequency band of interest to UK/EU Helium Hotspot operators – the UK/EU 868 frequency band.
Caoboth 2026-03-05 11:29 a.m.
22 is the limit on a heltec v3 30 is on v4
mrlm 2026-03-05 11:29 a.m.
Not it the software it isnt. 22 is the limit of the SX1262 chip, the radiolib code for some reason accepts more and doesn't tell you but the chip ignores it. On a v4 and some other boards there is an extra PA upping it to 28+-1. It is very confusing and doesn't need to be but the argumant was that the software cannot possibly know how all the boards work so it is up the the user to do the conversion. (edited)
Caoboth 2026-03-05 11:33 a.m.
tbh fella it seems to be working well i got to admit , but you could well be correct
mrlm 2026-03-05 11:35 a.m.
https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/main/docs/faq.md If you went straight from 10 to 30, it is probably set to 10 still. This makes it 22 after the PA the same as all the other boards. Power isn't everything, if you have line of sight it works with the smallest built in antenna which is emmiting less power in any given direction but the same total over all. (edited)
Caoboth 2026-03-05 12:01 p.m.
On plus side though getting some nice zero hop neighbours
1
mrlm
Not it the software it isnt. 22 is the limit of the SX1262 chip, the radiolib code for some reason accepts more and doesn't tell you but the chip ignores it. On a v4 and some other boards there is an extra PA upping it to 28+-1. It is very confusing and doesn't need to be but the argumant was that the software cannot possibly know how all the boards work so it is up the the user to do the conversion. (edited)
NE35 2026-03-05 12:17 p.m.
Is it right that setting a V4 to 22 is in reality 28-30 because the setting is for the chip and the extra boost is added after the chip output on a hardware level?
12:17 p.m.
Yeah it's in the pic you shared. Just checking before mine arrives. (edited)
mrlm 2026-03-05 12:19 p.m.
Yes, I sufferered the lack of documentation and confusing wording in the app months ago so you dont need to
1
NE35 2026-03-05 12:19 p.m.

NE35 2026-03-06 11:39 a.m.
Forwarded
MeshCore Firmware v1.14.0 Scott has posted a writeup on his blog about the new multi-byte path support in this release.
  • https://buymeacoffee.com/ripplebiz/path-diagnostics-improvements Companion Firmware
  • https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/releases/tag/companion-v1.14.0
  • multibyte path hash support
  • new 'auto add max hops' preference
  • new radio AGC reset implementation
  • HeltecV4 power fixes
  • RAK3401 power optimisations
  • Heltec Tracker V2 power fixes Repeater Firmware
  • https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/releases/tag/repeater-v1.14.0
  • multibyte path hash support
  • new CLI command: "get/set loop.detect …"
  • new CLI command: "get/set path.hash.mode …"
  • new CLI command: "get bootloader.ver"
  • new CLI command: "discover.neighbors"
  • new radio AGC reset implementation
  • HeltecV4 power fixes
  • RAK3401 power optimisations
  • Heltec Tracker V2 power fixes Room Server Firmware
  • https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/releases/tag/room-server-v1.14.0
  • multibyte path hash support
  • new radio AGC reset implementation
  • HeltecV4 power fixes
  • RAK3401 power optimisations
  • Heltec Tracker V2 power fixes

868Mhz tuned 8.5dBi antenna for HNT and LoRa
Extend the range of your Helium hotspot with our tuned LoRa / HNT antenna, for outdoor use. Designed in the United Kingdom from aviation-grade components, and provides excellent range for HNT (Helium) hotspots as well as for aviation (FLARM or OGN) applications. Compatible antenna for all Helium miners - Bobcat, RAK, and Nebra miner antenna.

Fireball 2026-03-07 10:55 a.m.
have you got any use for a companion linked up to mqtt or is it better on the repeaters?
Bye Andy 2026-03-07 10:56 a.m.
just repeaters for now, ill look at getting companions on it as well but that would be in future I think. the main issue with companions is the IATA codes would change if you go on holiday to like cornwall and the system expects a fixed position right now (edited)
Fireball 2026-03-07 10:56 a.m.
ah fair play, i've got a spare v3 sat here thats all so could have chucked it on for some more info if needed, not that it'd make much different considering how close we are
Bye Andy 2026-03-07 10:57 a.m.
yeah the repeaters that are close are used as 'validation' repeaters, if it sees its in the same IATA area it will use it to check paths etc
1
11:00 a.m.
I havent tested it yet but in theory if we have say 2 repeaters on MQTT per IATA code it will follow the packet hash along as it jumps through the air I guess and the fixed MQTT repeaters we have can be set as 'known good' locations to make the branch predictions better

5:37 p.m.
The new repeater code is backwards compatible, the new multibyte path method is not.
NE35 2026-03-07 5:37 p.m.
Because if defaults to 1 that's what they could have been using backwards compatible for too. Meaning if you flash it and don't change anything it will still work

Yes, not everyone gets that is what is meant. (edited)

NE35 2026-03-08 6:13 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-08 6:14 p.m.
For full power you want either a ceramic bandpass filter or a cavity filter. https://acasom.com/products/868mhz-7-types-cavity-filter-for-helium-network-filter-waterproof-lora-indoor-use-high-out-band-rejection
868MHz 7 Types High-Rejection Cavity Filters for EU Helium & LoRaWA...
DESCRIPTION ACASOM offers seven specialized types of 868MHz Cavity Filters to meet the exact demands of European Helium Network and LoRaWAN deployments. Understanding the core need is crucial: Why is a high out-of-band rejection filter non-negotiable? The primary function of a filter is to eliminate interference signal
NE35 2026-03-08 6:15 p.m.

Bye Andy 2026-03-08 10:23 p.m.
wow its very late but thats the longest hop message ive ever decoded with high confidence
10:23 p.m.
thats 14 hops
10:26 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-09 6:51 a.m.
Annoyingly I changed my repeated ID from 66 to 77, as there were three colissions within 30 miles. It was at the time the best choice from analysis of the ID frequency of use vs location data. Then someone else did the same 26 miles away.
Gallie_2k 2026-03-09 7:20 a.m.
Typical thankfully at least it can be changed now via the mesh instead of having to mess about flipping it to a companion to change the code
mrlm 2026-03-09 8:22 a.m.
I was reluctant to change it as i have been carful to backup all keys from the start, including repeaters before it was possible to do with commands. It annoys me when people change something and create three new keys which get advertised out. (edited)
Bye Andy
wow its very late but thats the longest hop message ive ever decoded with high confidence
mrlm 2026-03-09 8:27 a.m.
It looks fine to me. York GH is very prominent in flat land, I know it can reach crows nest 2 in the peaks. I am in the shadow of the hill to that side so I would get it from there and share east west. Crows nest 2 to 5 cross the pennines, from there is it flat to Wrexham and south is a mistery to me.

Gallie_2k 2026-03-09 2:11 p.m.
Surgestions of improvements? Currently got a 3dBi fibre antenna thinking it would give me more signal but messages are taking 3 goes to get through.
2:12 p.m.
Old version
2:12 p.m.
New one
mrlm 2026-03-09 2:18 p.m.
Not with the antenna but when I bought thos d5 enclosures I had issues with charging the batteries, tunred out the metal contact on the positive side were making a poor connection to the batteries. I bent them all outwards a bit.
Gallie_2k 2026-03-09 2:19 p.m.
No worries I'll keep a eye in it not charing it has been going from 81% lowest to 100% before I took it down
mrlm 2026-03-09 2:20 p.m.
Oh it will go to 100%, of the one battery connected.
2:21 p.m.
Some 18650 are slightly different length, and some have a bulge on top. Mine were flat.
2:21 p.m.
You can tell by the friction if you rotate the battery
Gallie_2k 2026-03-09 2:23 p.m.
Should have checked with a multimeter before I put it back up
mrlm 2026-03-09 2:23 p.m.
On the connecting cable, make sure the SMA side is not RP-SMA
Gallie_2k
Surgestions of improvements? Currently got a 3dBi fibre antenna thinking it would give me more signal but messages are taking 3 goes to get through.

mrlm 2026-03-09 2:40 p.m.
A multimeter wont help, the combined voltage is the average of them (V1+V2+Vn)/n (edited)
2:40 p.m.
My Ziisor came with RP-SMA cable, unscrew it and check one side has a pin the other a hole, RP-SMA swaps side so you end up with two holes and no connection. Its not a bad antenna or cable though. (edited)

NE35 2026-03-09 3:13 p.m.
Is it just gadgets own firmware that needs a code change? Is it already in the official firmware?
Bye Andy 2026-03-09 3:33 p.m.
no the official firmware is broken as well
mrlm 2026-03-09 3:47 p.m.
Sorry missed this, the board has a small but significant difference so it just worn work yet. They have swapped the FEM from GC1109 to KCT8103L so the RF switch is a different pin new board is stuck in RX mode. But it can do more, like turning off the extra LNA. I found these https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1867 https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1857 And this flasher with the latest dev + the fix https://mcimages.weebl.me/?commitId=heltec_v4.3 If you try to transmit without it you probably wont hurt anything though. (edited)
Fireball 2026-03-09 4:50 p.m.

NE35 2026-03-09 4:53 p.m.

Fireball
well that was strange, just reconnected to my companion and watched it delete all my contacts in real time, including favourites
mrlm 2026-03-09 4:53 p.m.
This is where you learn about backups, and why you should regularly do them with these devices. Was it ESP or nRF?
NE35 2026-03-09 4:54 p.m.
Esp when it happened to me, on a V3
Fireball 2026-03-09 4:55 p.m.
aye there's nothing special on there so i dont bother backing it up, its more of a tinker toy than anything important so no great loss if it wipes, stuff i actually rely on gets backed up tho. This is on a heltec meshpocket which is nrf i believe
mrlm 2026-03-09 4:56 p.m.
nRF has limited space and the bluetooth messes up often. ESP was pretty reliable till the recent bug of orphaned files filling up the storage. Either way the fix when it happens is to erase and restore a backup. just in case there is file corruption.

mrlm 2026-03-11 3:14 p.m.

mrlm 2026-03-11 3:16 p.m.

3:20 p.m.
I wouldn't get too caught up with the gain of the antenna. Obviously it has uses but I have proven that tiny cards like the T1000E and Rak Tag can communicate 20 miles with line of sight. Their antennas have very little gain as they need to work in all orientations.

868Mhz tuned 8.5dBi antenna for HNT and LoRa
Extend the range of your Helium hotspot with our tuned LoRa / HNT antenna, for outdoor use. Designed in the United Kingdom from aviation-grade components, and provides excellent range for HNT (Helium) hotspots as well as for aviation (FLARM or OGN) applications. Compatible antenna for all Helium miners - Bobcat, RAK, and Nebra miner antenna.

1:51 p.m.

1:51 p.m.
you should be seeing two MQTT brokers not the 1
1:52 p.m.

I think it isnt storing the retain status properly
NE35 2026-03-13 1:53 p.m.
It keeps my status after I flashed and showed all the brokers, I put the info in for both.
Bye Andy 2026-03-13 1:53 p.m.
just checking to see if it fixed it for me
1:53 p.m.
but I think it has
1:53 p.m.
yeah it has
1:54 p.m.
I know what the issue is, when you do retain status it doesnt send the MQTT payload over properly
1:54 p.m.
so quick fix, turn off retain status, add the optional broker with the same details as broker 1 and use this as the custom topic meshcore/MME/<PUBLIC_KEY>/status
1:54 p.m.
ill fix it in the background in a sec
1:55 p.m.
in the serial log after you just click apply MQTT you should see something like
1:55 p.m.
17:53:03[rx] MQTT reporter: broker 2 connected 17:53:03[rx] MQTT reporter: broker 1 connected
NE35 2026-03-13 1:57 p.m.

Bye Andy 2026-03-13 1:58 p.m.
1:58 p.m.
should look like that
NE35 2026-03-13 1:58 p.m.

Bye Andy 2026-03-13 1:59 p.m.
you dont need meshrank in optional
1:59 p.m.
thats where you put status for me
1:59 p.m.
meshrank goes in broker pair 3
1:59 p.m.
just dont enable 'enable optional broker' for pair 3

you cant use meshcore defaults for that one

the server can handle them, the frontend cant lol
8:36 p.m.
thats what im trying to improve rn so it can handle more clients
8:36 p.m.
I really want a live packet tracer but im starting to think its impossible with the way I have it right now as if we were to have like 100 repeaters like meshrank does thats a lot to compute and backtrace per packet
1
NE35 2026-03-13 8:38 p.m.
Even 10 observers it would have too many paths recorded to show at once and would need something to determine which one to show. Probably the longest (edited)
Bye Andy 2026-03-13 8:38 p.m.
yeah the way it works now is it takes the MQTT observer that recieved the packet and uses that as the starting point then works backwards
8:38 p.m.
if another node recieves it, it compares the paths and computes again with the new data
8:39 p.m.
its why you see it if LBA recieves a packet then like a second later we do it recomputes the entire path
NE35 2026-03-13 8:40 p.m.
I've seen it change in the list from lba to all a few times yeah
8:40 p.m.
Or show a path then change it
Bye Andy 2026-03-13 8:40 p.m.
yeah its because its more confident in the new path compared to the old one with the new data that was provided
NE35 2026-03-13 8:41 p.m.
If you get more you may have to disable that and accept a little innacuracy for load balance (edited)
Bye Andy 2026-03-13 8:41 p.m.
that red path is due to a repeater in durham using the same 2 hex public key start as me, I cant for the life of me get it to change that
8:41 p.m.
its because I have the test repeater so my gadgetrepeater is seeing its recieved packet twice and the packet logic sees it as another hop
Bye Andy
its because I have the test repeater so my gadgetrepeater is seeing its recieved packet twice and the packet logic sees it as another hop

1:47 a.m.
Shiremoor Repeater c5bbd48cf4534eb622544647821d9fb5aaed364dbc01916badd892e5ae896e73 M6IRO Repeater 67cefb69661fe80e433f06c804ba4da4f680261decc86a6a772696492742f417 DINNINGTON-NE13-SOUTH 8b84803d31a175d01756e036cc42ebc9e0ca580b0dc4a3b229f5fc72885efd0f DINNINGTON-NE13-OMNI 7b3f9fa8927eeb1af952fb22c7f8036323d0d833f3d0359528ab4fef1ba2f521
1:52 a.m.

odd.beat 2026-04-03 7:57 p.m.
I haven’t been able to send any messages to contacts or in public. Everything I receive seems to come through the repeater called Sh@DoW-Unit-SIMONSIDE-O. is it a case that the repeater has strong enough signal to get to me but my heltec v4 isn’t strong enough to get a signal back to the repeater?
jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt) 2026-04-04 2:24 a.m.
Simonside is something of an anomaly. It's over 1000 feet above sea level so it really does punch above it's weight. It's only a 5.5 dBi gain antenna (If I remember correctly) but as has been said already... Height is might
